skill.name: micro-saas-launcher
skill.description: Expert in launching small, focused SaaS products fast - the indie hacker approach to building profitable software. Covers idea validation, MVP development, pricing, launch strategies, and growing traction.
skill.summary: Launch profitable micro-SaaS products in weeks using proven indie hacker frameworks for validation, MVP speed, and sustainable growth.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains documentation-only content with no executable code. All 21 static analysis findings are false positives - the detected patterns appear within markdown code blocks that serve as illustrative examples, not actual runnable code. No scripts, network calls, filesystem operations, or command execution present.

content.value_statement: Building a SaaS product often takes too long and fails to validate market demand. This skill provides proven frameworks to validate ideas, ship MVPs quickly, and grow profitable micro-SaaS businesses using indie hacker strategies.
actual_capabilities: Validate SaaS ideas using structured frameworks before building
Define MVP scope with 2-week development timelines
Design pricing strategies for micro-SaaS products
Create launch playbooks for early customer acquisition
Identify and avoid common micro-SaaS anti-patterns
limitations: Does not execute code or deploy applications
Does not directly integrate with external services like Stripe or Supabase
Provides guidance and frameworks, not turnkey solutions
Does not replace legal or financial professional advice

use_cases.title: First-Time SaaS Founder
use_cases.description: A developer with a product idea needs structured guidance on validating demand and building an MVP quickly without wasting months on features users do not want.

use_cases.title: Side Project to Business
use_cases.description: An indie hacker with a side project wants to transition to a profitable micro-SaaS by implementing proper pricing, launch strategies, and growth tactics.
use_cases.target_user: Side project builder transitioning to entrepreneurship

use_cases.title: Rapid Market Testing
use_cases.description: An experienced founder wants to quickly test multiple SaaS ideas using lean validation methods to find the most promising opportunity before committing resources.

prompt_templates.title: Validate My SaaS Idea
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an idea for [describe your SaaS concept]. Help me validate this idea using the validation framework. Guide me through answering: does the problem exist, will people pay, can I build it, and can I reach customers?
prompt_templates.scenario: Early stage idea validation before writing any code

prompt_templates.title: Plan My 2-Week MVP
prompt_templates.prompt: My SaaS does [core function]. Help me scope a 2-week MVP using the recommended stack. Break down what to build each day, what features to include, and what to skip for later iterations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Ready to build after validation, need structured development plan

prompt_templates.title: Design My Pricing Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: My micro-SaaS targets [customer type] and solves [specific problem]. The alternative costs them [time/money]. Recommend pricing tiers and price points using the micro-SaaS pricing framework.
prompt_templates.scenario: MVP is ready, need to set prices before launch

prompt_templates.title: Create My Launch Playbook
prompt_templates.prompt: I am launching my micro-SaaS in [industry/niche]. My MVP does [core feature]. Create a launch playbook covering: pre-launch activities, launch day tasks, and post-launch growth tactics for the first 30 days.
prompt_templates.scenario: MVP complete, preparing for public launch and customer acquisition

output_examples.input: I want to build a tool that helps freelancers track billable hours across multiple clients
output_examples.output: Validation priority: Talk to 5+ freelancers about their current time-tracking workflow. Key questions: What do they use now? What frustrates them? Would they pay $15-29/month for a better solution? Competitor analysis: Existing tools like Toggl, Harvest, Clockify dominate - your differentiator must be specific to freelancer workflows, not generic time tracking.

output_examples.input: My MVP scope feels too big. I am building a project management tool with tasks, boards, timeline, reports, and integrations.
output_examples.output: MVP reduction: Keep ONLY tasks and boards. Remove timeline, reports, and integrations for v1. Your 2-week MVP: Week 1 - Build task creation, assignment, and board view. Week 2 - Add Stripe checkout, landing page, and basic onboarding. Launch with just these features. Add timeline and reports only if users specifically request them after using v1.

best_practices: Validate with money before building - pre-sell or find paying beta users
Ship MVP in 2 weeks maximum - extend timeline only for critical bugs
Start with higher pricing - discount later rather than raise prices
Build distribution before product - know how you will reach customers
Focus on one core feature - resist adding features until users request them
anti_patterns: Building in secret without user feedback leads to wasted development on unwanted features
Adding features before shipping the core functionality delays launch and revenue
Pricing too low attracts price-sensitive customers and makes business sustainability difficult
Choosing complex technology stacks increases development time and maintenance burden

faq.question: How long should I spend validating before building?
faq.answer: Spend 1-2 weeks on validation maximum. Talk to 5-10 potential users, attempt pre-sales, and research competitors. If you cannot find evidence of demand in 2 weeks, pivot to a different idea rather than continuing to validate indefinitely.

faq.question: What is a realistic revenue goal for a micro-SaaS?
faq.answer: A successful micro-SaaS typically generates $1,000-$10,000 MRR within the first year. Focus on profitability over growth - a solo founder can live well on $5,000 MRR with minimal expenses. Aim for 10-20 paying customers at $50-100/month rather than thousands of free users.

faq.question: Should I offer a free tier or free trial?
faq.answer: For micro-SaaS, a 7-14 day free trial converts better than a free tier. Free tiers attract non-paying users and increase support burden. A trial creates urgency and lets users experience full value. Exception: Offer free tier if your product benefits from network effects or viral growth.

faq.question: How do I choose the right technology stack?
faq.answer: Use established, boring technology you already know. For most solo founders: Next.js for full-stack, Supabase for database and auth, Stripe for payments, Vercel for hosting. Avoid learning new frameworks during MVP development. Your stack choice matters less than shipping quickly.

faq.question: What if a competitor already exists in my space?
faq.answer: Competition validates market demand. Most successful micro-SaaS products have competitors. Your advantage: faster iteration, better customer service, niche focus, or superior user experience. Study competitor reviews to find complaints you can solve. Do not compete on price - compete on specificity and service.

faq.question: How much should I charge for my micro-SaaS?
faq.answer: Price at 20-50% of the alternative cost. If your tool saves 5 hours monthly at $50/hour, charge $50-125/month. Typical ranges: simple tools $9-29/month, pro tools $29-99/month, B2B tools $49-299/month. Start higher than comfortable - you can discount for early adopters but raising prices later is difficult.
